title: Semantic search - Use custom models - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: Semantic Search
extractedEntities.description: Ontology capability that enables natural language queries against Object Types by comparing vector embeddings of query text with pre-computed embeddings stored as vector properties on objects.

extractedEntities.name: Vector Property
extractedEntities.description: A special property type on Object Types that stores float array embeddings generated by models, enabling KNN-based semantic similarity search across objects.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology entity that represents a real-world concept (e.g., Document) backed by a dataset, with properties including vector embeddings for semantic search.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: A named attribute on an Object Type, such as Document_Content or Link, that stores data values including text and vector embeddings.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Executable code registered in the Ontology that can be used to query objects via semantic search using KNN operations on vector properties.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's core data platform that hosts modeling objectives, data transforms, and the Ontology where vector-enabled Object Types are created and queried.

extractedEntities.name: KNN Functions on Objects
extractedEntities.description: K-Nearest Neighbors search capability within Foundry that finds the closest objects by comparing vector embeddings, used as the underlying mechanism for semantic search.

extractedEntities.name: Embedding Transform
extractedEntities.description: A data pipeline transform that runs input data through an embedding model (e.g., all-MiniLM-L6-v2) and casts output double arrays to float arrays for storage as vector properties.

extractedEntities.name: Live Modeling Deployment
extractedEntities.description: A runtime deployment of a model in Foundry that enables real-time embedding generation for queries against the semantic search index.

extractedEntities.name: BYOM Semantic Search
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of bringing your own embedding model to Foundry, running transforms to generate embeddings, storing them as vector properties, and querying via KNN Functions.

keyInsights: Semantic search in Foundry works by storing vector embeddings as a special vector property type on Object Types, then using KNN Functions to find nearest neighbors at query time.
Custom embedding models must output float arrays compatible with the Foundry Ontology vector type; double arrays from models need to be cast to floats in the embedding transform.
The BYOM workflow is no longer recommended in favor of Palantir-provided models, but the architecture reveals the underlying vector storage and KNN query mechanism.
Each Object Type participating in semantic search requires both a backing dataset with content and a vector property populated by an embedding pipeline.
Functions serve as the query interface for semantic search, wrapping KNN operations and making them accessible through the Ontology's standard function execution model.

title: Semantic search - Document processing - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: Document Chunking
extractedEntities.description: The process of breaking large text documents into smaller semantic chunks, each represented as a separate Ontology object linked back to the original document object for granular retrieval.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology entity used to represent both source documents and their derived chunks, with link relationships between parent document and child chunk objects.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology relationship connecting chunk objects back to their parent document objects, enabling navigation from search results to source documents.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Named attributes on chunk objects including object_id, object_text, chunk_text, and chunk_index that store the segmented content and ordering metadata.

extractedEntities.name: Pipeline Builder
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's no-code data transformation tool used to parse PDFs, extract text, and apply chunking strategies using boards like Chunk String, Explode Array, and Add Row Number.

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's application builder used to surface semantic search results alongside rendered PDFs for source-of-truth cross-validation by users.

extractedEntities.name: Chunk String Board
extractedEntities.description: A Pipeline Builder board that segments text into smaller pieces using configurable chunk size, separator priority, and overlap parameters.

extractedEntities.name: Media Set
extractedEntities.description: A Foundry data storage mechanism for importing and storing PDF documents and images before text extraction and chunking.

extractedEntities.name: Document-to-Chunk Pipeline
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where PDFs are imported as media sets, text is extracted, chunked into smaller segments, exploded into separate rows, and each chunk becomes its own Ontology object linked to the parent document.

extractedEntities.name: ObjectSet
extractedEntities.description: A filtered collection of chunk objects that results from a semantic search query, displayed alongside the original document for user validation.

keyInsights: Semantic search accuracy improves significantly when large documents are chunked into smaller pieces because embedding models have maximum input lengths and smaller text segments are more semantically distinct.
The chunking pipeline produces a one-to-many relationship: each source document Object maps to many chunk Objects via Link Types, preserving document provenance.

Pipeline Builder provides no-code chunking via the Chunk String board with configurable chunk size, separator priority hierarchy, and overlap parameters for maintaining context across chunk boundaries.
The recommended UX pattern surfaces search results alongside the rendered source PDF, enabling human cross-validation of AI-retrieved information.
Chunk objects carry metadata (chunk_index, parent object_id) that enables reassembly and ordering of results back into their original document context.

title: Building with Palantir AIP: Semantic Search - Palantir Blog

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that enables building end-to-end semantic search applications from unstructured data to interactive AI copilots in minutes.

extractedEntities.name: Virtual Tables
extractedEntities.description: AIP data connectivity feature that enables registering data assets from external cloud platforms (e.g., BigQuery) without duplicating data, allowing immediate building on existing data.

extractedEntities.name: Pipeline Builder
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's flagship data pipelining application providing low/no-code pipeline creation on an auto-scaling Spark and Flink backend with git-style branching.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Logic
extractedEntities.description: No-code tool for constructing LLM-powered functions that can combine semantic search with other Ontology operations for complex AI workflows.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Assist
extractedEntities.description: Conversational interface that allows users to interact with semantic search results and Ontology data through natural language, deployed within Workshop applications.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology-Powered Vector Store
extractedEntities.description: The Ontology's built-in capability to store and query vector embeddings on Object Types, serving as an enterprise vector database integrated with access controls.

extractedEntities.name: Semantic Search
extractedEntities.description: Search capability that finds results based on meaning of words and phrases rather than keyword matching, powered by embeddings stored in the Ontology vector store.

extractedEntities.name: Embeddings
extractedEntities.description: Numerical vector representations of text that capture semantic meaning, where numerically similar vectors indicate semantically similar content (e.g., 'face mask' close to 'face covering').

extractedEntities.name: Unstructured-to-Copilot Pipeline
extractedEntities.description: End-to-end pattern: connect external data via Virtual Tables, generate embeddings in Pipeline Builder, store in Ontology vector store, search via AIP Logic, interact via AIP Assist.

extractedEntities.name: Enterprise Trouble Ticket Search
extractedEntities.description: Semantic search use case converting trouble ticket text into embeddings for finding similar past issues and resolutions across an enterprise ticketing system.

keyInsights: AIP's semantic search stack consists of seven sequential components: Virtual Tables for data connectivity, Embeddings for vectorization, Pipeline Builder for transformation, Ontology vector store for persistence, semantic search for querying, AIP Logic for orchestration, and AIP Assist for user interaction.
Virtual Tables eliminate data duplication by registering external cloud data assets (BigQuery, etc.) directly in AIP, enabling immediate building without ETL delays.
The Ontology serves as an enterprise vector store where embeddings are stored alongside structured data and governed by the same access controls as all other Ontology data.
AIP enables going from raw unstructured data to a production-grade semantic search application in minutes, not months, by composing existing platform capabilities.
Semantic similarity is demonstrated through embedding proximity: numerically alike embedding vectors indicate semantic similarity between concepts.

title: Pipeline Builder - Transforms - Use LLM node - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: Pipeline Builder
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's data pipelining application that includes a Use LLM node for no-code LLM execution on data at scale within transformation pipelines.

extractedEntities.name: Use LLM Node
extractedEntities.description: A Pipeline Builder node that executes LLMs on dataset columns at scale, with pre-engineered prompt templates for classification, summarization, translation, sentiment analysis, extraction, and custom prompts.

extractedEntities.name: Classification Prompt Template
extractedEntities.description: Pre-engineered LLM prompt template that categorizes data into user-defined categories with configurable multiplicity (one, multiple, or exact number of categories).

extractedEntities.name: Summarization Prompt Template
extractedEntities.description: Pre-engineered LLM prompt template that summarizes data to a configurable length measured in words, sentences, or paragraphs.

extractedEntities.name: Translation Prompt Template
extractedEntities.description: Pre-engineered LLM prompt template that translates data columns into a specified target language.

extractedEntities.name: Sentiment Analysis Prompt Template
extractedEntities.description: Pre-engineered LLM prompt template that assigns numeric scores to data based on positive or negative sentiment.

extractedEntities.name: Extraction Prompt Template
extractedEntities.description: Pre-engineered LLM prompt template that extracts specific entities or information from text data columns.

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform whose capabilities must be granted by a platform administrator before users can access the Use LLM node in Pipeline Builder.

extractedEntities.name: No-Code LLM Data Enrichment
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of using pre-engineered prompt templates in Pipeline Builder to enrich datasets with LLM-generated classifications, summaries, translations, and sentiment scores without writing code.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Capabilities Permission
extractedEntities.description: Platform administrator permission required to grant users access to AIP features including the Use LLM node in Pipeline Builder for custom workflows.

keyInsights: The Use LLM node integrates LLM processing directly into data transformation pipelines, enabling LLM-enriched columns to flow through to Ontology Object Type properties without separate infrastructure.
Pre-engineered prompt templates (classification, summarization, translation, sentiment, extraction) encode prompt engineering expertise, lowering the barrier for non-technical users.
The trial/preview functionality allows iterating on prompts using a few rows before committing to full dataset execution, providing a fast feedback loop for prompt engineering.
Access to LLM nodes requires explicit AIP capability permissions granted by platform administrators, maintaining security governance over AI feature usage.
LLM node output becomes standard dataset columns that can feed into subsequent Pipeline Builder transformations, enabling chaining of AI enrichment with traditional data processing.

title: Foundry platform summary for LLMs - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's core data and operational platform whose architecture, terminology, and integration patterns are summarized for LLM and AI agent consumption.

extractedEntities.name: Palantir MCP
extractedEntities.description: Model Context Protocol integration that allows AI agents to use tools like search_foundry_documentation to explore Palantir documentation programmatically.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology entity representing real-world concepts, defined in the platform summary as a fundamental building block covered in the LLM context document.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Named attribute on Object Types, covered as core terminology in the platform summary for LLM understanding.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology relationship type connecting Object Types, included as core terminology in the LLM platform summary.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology mechanism for writing changes, covered in the platform summary as part of the Ontology's operational capabilities.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Executable computation registered in the Ontology, included in the platform summary as a key Ontology concept.

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: Ontology abstraction mechanism covered in the platform summary, enabling polymorphic behavior across Object Types.

extractedEntities.name: LLM Platform Context Injection
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of providing a pre-built, engineer-reviewed platform summary to LLMs and AI agents as context, enabling them to reason about Foundry concepts accurately.

extractedEntities.name: search_foundry_documentation Tool
extractedEntities.description: A Palantir MCP tool that allows AI agents to search Foundry documentation, complementing the static platform summary with dynamic documentation exploration.

keyInsights: Palantir provides an official, engineer-reviewed platform summary specifically designed for LLM consumption, recognizing that AI agents need structured context about the platform to operate effectively.
The platform summary covers core architecture, terminology, and data integration patterns -- essentially a machine-readable Foundry primer that can be copy-pasted into AI agent conversations.
Palantir MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration enables AI agents to dynamically search Foundry documentation beyond the static summary, creating a two-tier context system.
The summary was last updated December 19, 2025, and is maintained by Palantir engineers, establishing it as an authoritative reference for AI agent interactions with the platform.
This page represents Palantir's strategy of making the Ontology self-describing to AI systems, enabling AI agents to understand and operate within the platform without human intermediaries.

title: Bring your own model - Register an LLM using function interfaces - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: Function Interface
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology abstraction that defines a standard contract (e.g., ChatCompletion) for functions, enabling custom LLM implementations to be used interchangeably across all Palantir developer products.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: TypeScript executable registered in the Ontology that implements a Function Interface (e.g., ChatCompletion) and calls external LLM APIs via webhooks.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Logic
extractedEntities.description: Palantir product that can natively consume functions implementing the ChatCompletion interface, enabling custom LLMs to be used in no-code AI workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Pipeline Builder
extractedEntities.description: Palantir data pipeline tool that can consume custom LLMs registered via ChatCompletion function interfaces for data transformation tasks.

extractedEntities.name: Agent Studio
extractedEntities.description: Palantir product for building AI agents that can use custom LLMs registered through function interfaces.

extractedEntities.name: Data Connection
extractedEntities.description: Palantir application for creating REST API sources, webhooks, and network policies that enable secure communication with external LLM endpoints.

extractedEntities.name: ChatCompletion Interface
extractedEntities.description: A specific Function Interface contract that defines the standard input/output shape for LLM chat completion, enabling any conforming function to be used as an LLM across the platform.

extractedEntities.name: Webhook
extractedEntities.description: A Data Connection construct that wraps external API calls (e.g., to OpenAI) for secure invocation from TypeScript functions, maintaining platform security boundaries.

extractedEntities.name: BYOM via Function Interface
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of bringing your own LLM by implementing the ChatCompletion function interface in TypeScript, calling the external model via a webhook, and registering it for cross-platform use.

extractedEntities.name: Network Policy
extractedEntities.description: Data Connection security control that governs which external endpoints (e.g., api.openai.com) can be reached by webhooks, ensuring all external model calls are explicitly authorized.

keyInsights: Function Interfaces are the key abstraction enabling BYOM (Bring Your Own Model): any LLM that implements the ChatCompletion interface becomes usable across AIP Logic, Pipeline Builder, Agent Studio, and Workshop.

The BYOM architecture uses a three-layer approach: Data Connection source defines the external endpoint, a webhook wraps the API call securely, and a TypeScript function implements the ChatCompletion interface.
Custom LLMs can be hosted on-premise, in the customer's own cloud, or fine-tuned on another platform -- the Function Interface abstraction decouples model location from platform consumption.

Two integration methods exist: webhook-based (TypeScript calls webhook directly) and direct source (REST API source in Data Connection with fetch calls), both producing the same ChatCompletion-compatible function.
Network policies and bearer token authentication in Data Connection ensure that all external LLM API calls are explicitly authorized and secured at the platform level.

title: Functions - Models - Language models within functions - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: TypeScript or Python executable registered in the Ontology that can import and invoke Palantir-provided language models for tasks like sentiment analysis, chat completion, and vision processing.

extractedEntities.name: Language Model Import
extractedEntities.description: The mechanism for importing Palantir-provided LLMs into a code repository via the Model Imports side panel, generating strongly-typed code bindings (e.g., Gpt41 class).

extractedEntities.name: createChatCompletion
extractedEntities.description: Generated method on imported language model classes for standard text-based chat completion with system and user message parameters.

extractedEntities.name: createChatVisionCompletion
extractedEntities.description: Generated method on imported language model classes for multimodal chat completion accepting both text and image inputs (Attachments).

extractedEntities.name: createChatCompletionStreamed
extractedEntities.description: Generated method on imported language model classes for streaming chat completion responses, enabling real-time output processing.

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that must be enabled on an enrollment before Palantir-provided language models can be used within functions.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: The platform hosting code repositories where functions are authored and where language model code bindings are auto-generated after import.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entity whose objects can be passed to language model functions for AI-powered analysis, enrichment, or decision-making.

extractedEntities.name: LLM-Powered Function Pattern
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of importing a language model into a function repository, using generated code bindings to call the model with structured prompts, and exposing the result as an Ontology function.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Builder Capabilities
extractedEntities.description: Permission that must be granted to users before they can use Palantir-provided language models within functions, controlling access to AI capabilities.

keyInsights: Language models are integrated into functions via auto-generated strongly-typed code bindings (e.g., the Gpt41 class with typed methods), providing compile-time safety for LLM interactions.

Three distinct API methods are generated per model: createChatCompletion (text), createChatVisionCompletion (multimodal with images), and createChatCompletionStreamed (streaming), covering the major LLM interaction modes.
The import mechanism uses a Model Imports side panel that generates code bindings automatically via task runner, abstracting away the complexity of model endpoint management.
Functions using language models follow a system prompt + user prompt pattern, with the function wrapping the LLM call and returning structured output that the Ontology can consume.
Both TypeScript and Python are supported for authoring LLM-powered functions, with the same model import and code generation workflow available in both languages.

title: AIP Logic - Blocks - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: AIP Logic
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's no-code AI function builder where functions are composed of blocks that chain together discrete data interactions, LLM calls, and Ontology operations.

extractedEntities.name: Use LLM Block
extractedEntities.description: The core AIP Logic block that enables LLM interaction via prompts, tools, and structured outputs, supporting any available LLM in the platform (k-LLM philosophy).

extractedEntities.name: Apply Action Tool
extractedEntities.description: An AIP Logic tool that enables LLMs to use Action Types to edit the Ontology, with descriptions of when the LLM should invoke each action.

extractedEntities.name: Call Function Tool
extractedEntities.description: An AIP Logic tool that allows LLMs to invoke registered functions including other Logic functions, extending capabilities through function composition.

extractedEntities.name: Query Objects Tool
extractedEntities.description: An AIP Logic tool that gives LLMs read access to specified Object Types and their properties for data retrieval and reasoning.

extractedEntities.name: Calculator Tool
extractedEntities.description: An AIP Logic tool that provides LLMs with mathematical computation capabilities for numerical reasoning tasks.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology mechanism for writing changes that LLMs can invoke through the Apply Action tool in AIP Logic, enabling AI-driven Ontology modifications.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entity that LLMs can query through the Query Objects tool, with configurable property-level access and semantic search capabilities.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Executable computation that can be composed into AIP Logic blocks, enabling both code-defined and Logic-defined functions to be called by LLMs.

extractedEntities.name: Block Chaining
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of composing AIP Logic functions by connecting blocks sequentially, where each block's output becomes available as input to subsequent blocks.

keyInsights: AIP Logic functions are composed of blocks that chain together, with each block taking input, producing output, and performing a discrete operation -- creating a visual programming model for AI workflows.

LLMs in AIP Logic do not have direct access to tools; they can only request tool use, and AIP Logic executes those tool calls within the invoking user's permissions -- enforcing the security boundary.

The tool taxonomy in AIP Logic has four categories: Apply Actions (write to Ontology), Call Function (execute logic), Query Objects (read from Ontology), and Calculator (compute) -- covering CRUD + compute.
The k-LLM philosophy means AIP Logic supports any available LLM in the platform, avoiding vendor lock-in and enabling model selection per block based on task requirements.
Block types beyond LLM include loops, conditionals, apply action, execute function, and create variable -- making AIP Logic a full visual programming environment, not just an LLM wrapper.

title: AIP Logic - Automate AIP Logic - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: AIP Logic
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's no-code AI function builder whose functions can be automated to trigger on Ontology events, enabling AI-driven operational workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Automate
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's automation platform that monitors Object Sets and triggers AIP Logic functions when objects are created or match conditions, bridging AI reasoning to operational action.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology write operation that AIP Logic functions can output, which Automate either applies automatically or stages as proposals for human review.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entity that Automate monitors; new objects or object changes trigger AIP Logic execution, and Action Type outputs modify objects.

extractedEntities.name: Human-in-the-Loop AI Automation
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where AIP Logic generates Action proposals that are staged for human review before execution, with a 24-hour review window and per-user visibility.

extractedEntities.name: Event-Driven AI Automation
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where Automate monitors an Object Set and triggers AIP Logic for each new object, enabling automatic AI processing of incoming data.

extractedEntities.name: Agent Proposal
extractedEntities.description: A staged Action generated by an automated AIP Logic function that awaits human review, including the AI's reasoning (decision log) and the proposed Action preview.

extractedEntities.name: Agent Decision Log
extractedEntities.description: A detailed trace of the LLM's reasoning process visible during proposal review, enabling humans to understand and validate AI-generated Actions before approval.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: AIP Logic output that must return an Ontology edit (Action) for the Automation integration to function; the function's input must be an object for condition blocks.

keyInsights: AIP Logic + Automate creates event-driven AI: Automate monitors an Object Set, triggers AIP Logic per new object, and the AI either auto-applies or stages Actions for human review.
The human-in-the-loop pattern stages AI-generated Actions as proposals with a 24-hour review window, enabling human validation before Ontology changes are committed.
Agent Decision Logs provide full LLM reasoning transparency, allowing reviewers to inspect why the AI proposed a specific Action before approving it.
Two automation modes exist: automatic execution (AI applies Ontology edits directly) and staged proposals (AI suggests edits for human approval), configurable per automation.
AIP Logic functions must output an Ontology edit (Action) and accept an object as input for the Automate integration to work, establishing a clear contract between AI and automation.

title: Speedrun: Your First Agentic AIP Workflow - Palantir Learn

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform used to build agentic workflows where AI agents handle clinical trial recruitment processes autonomously with human teaming.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Logic
extractedEntities.description: Tool for creating AI agents that compose LLM prompts, Ontology inputs, and Action outputs into executable agentic functions.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Automate
extractedEntities.description: Automation platform that executes published AIP Logic agents on triggers like new object creation, enabling continuous automated AI processing.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Evals
extractedEntities.description: Evaluation framework for testing AIP Logic functions with configurable evaluators, test cases, and experiments to compare LLM performance.

extractedEntities.name: Marketplace
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's distribution platform for pre-built Ontology products that can be installed into a project, providing foundational Ontology structures.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology entity representing real-world concepts like Patient and Clinical Trial in the tutorial's recruitment use case.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology write mechanism that agents use to modify objects (e.g., updating patient eligibility status) based on LLM reasoning.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Published agent function that encapsulates the LLM logic, inputs, and outputs for execution by Automate or interactive use.

extractedEntities.name: Agentic Workflow Pattern
extractedEntities.description: End-to-end pattern: install foundational Ontology, create AIP Logic agent, evaluate with AIP Evals, publish as function, automate with AIP Automate, observe human-AI teaming.

extractedEntities.name: Clinical Trial Recruitment
extractedEntities.description: Use case where an AI agent identifies eligible patients for clinical trials by reasoning over patient data and trial criteria, automating the recruitment screening process.

keyInsights: The agentic workflow lifecycle has six stages: install foundational Ontology, create agent in AIP Logic, evaluate with AIP Evals, publish as function, automate execution, observe human-AI teaming.
AIP Evals enables systematic agent evaluation with configurable evaluators, test cases, and side-by-side LLM comparison experiments before deploying agents to production.
Marketplace products provide pre-built foundational Ontology structures that can be installed as starting points, accelerating development by reusing proven Object Type and Action definitions.
The human-AI teaming model has the AI agent performing initial screening and the automation observing effects, while humans retain oversight over the agent's operational decisions.
Agent publication as a function is the bridge between development (AIP Logic) and operations (AIP Automate), establishing a clear development-to-deployment pipeline for AI agents.

title: AI ethics and governance - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that integrates responsible AI capabilities throughout the development lifecycle, supporting eight core themes of ethical AI development.

extractedEntities.name: Sensitive Data Scanner
extractedEntities.description: AIP tool that automatically identifies protected attributes and potential bias sources in datasets for proactive fairness risk assessment.

extractedEntities.name: Modeling Objectives
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application supporting subset evaluation to systematically evaluate model performance across different demographic groups.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Evals
extractedEntities.description: Evaluation tool for testing AIP Logic function performance across diverse test cases with experiments to understand how input changes affect model responses.

extractedEntities.name: Data Health Monitoring
extractedEntities.description: Ongoing assessment tool for data representativeness and quality, identifying when datasets may under- or over-represent specific populations.

extractedEntities.name: AI Ethics Governance
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's framework of eight core responsible AI themes: equitable, robust, interpretable, privacy-preserving, safe, secure, accountable, and aligned.

extractedEntities.name: Bias Detection
extractedEntities.description: Platform capability for identifying sources of bias in data and evaluating models for unfair discrimination using Sensitive Data Scanner and subset evaluation.

extractedEntities.name: Responsible AI Lifecycle
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of integrating ethical AI considerations throughout the full system lifecycle from data foundations and pipelines to user interfaces and human decision-making workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: AIP Logic functions that are subject to ethical evaluation via AIP Evals, ensuring AI-driven functions meet fairness and quality standards.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology write operations that must be governed by ethical considerations, ensuring AI-triggered actions are fair and accountable.

keyInsights: Palantir's responsible AI framework has eight concrete themes: equitable, robust, interpretable, privacy-preserving, safe, secure, accountable, and aligned -- each with specific platform capabilities.

Bias detection operates at two levels: data-level (Sensitive Data Scanner, Data Health Monitoring) and model-level (subset evaluation, AIP Evals), providing defense-in-depth against unfair discrimination.
Responsible AI is positioned as integral to system design rather than a compliance afterthought, with capabilities embedded throughout the development lifecycle rather than bolted on.
AIP Evals serves double duty: it validates functional correctness of AI logic and enables ethical evaluation by testing across diverse demographic groups and input variations.
The implementation workflow progresses from data-level fairness assessment to model-level bias testing to mitigation strategies, creating a systematic process for ethical AI development.

title: AIP security and privacy - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that inherits all of Foundry's security measures including access controls, encryption, auditing, and adds AI-specific guardrails for LLM interactions.

extractedEntities.name: Agent Studio
extractedEntities.description: Palantir product for configuring AI agents where the principle of least privilege is enforced -- LLMs are granted access only to what is necessary for each task.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Guardrails
extractedEntities.description: Security controls governing every aspect of the AI system including what LLMs can access, how they interact with data, and what actions they can take.

extractedEntities.name: No Data Retention by Third Parties
extractedEntities.description: Technical and contractual guarantee that no customer data in prompts or completions is retained by third-party-hosted model service providers.

extractedEntities.name: No Model Retraining on Customer Data
extractedEntities.description: Guarantee that no customer data submitted in prompts or completions is used for retraining third-party-hosted models.

extractedEntities.name: Audit Trail
extractedEntities.description: Comprehensive audit capability tracking all AIP interactions, LLM prompts, completions, and actions for compliance and governance.

extractedEntities.name: Geographic Restrictions
extractedEntities.description: AIP uses regional endpoints where available (US, UK, EU) to minimize latency and comply with data residency requirements.

extractedEntities.name: Least Privilege for LLMs
extractedEntities.description: Agent Studio principle where LLMs are granted access only to the specific Ontology resources, tools, and data necessary for their assigned task.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's core platform whose security infrastructure (access controls, encryption, auditing) is inherited by AIP as the foundation for AI security.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entity protected by AIP's security measures including access controls that govern what LLMs can query and modify.

extractedRelationships.source: No Data Retention by Third Parties
No Model Retraining on Customer Data

keyInsights: AIP security is built on inheritance: all of Foundry's existing access controls, encryption, and auditing carry forward to AI interactions, rather than building a separate security model.
Third-party LLM providers have zero access to customer data -- no retention, no retraining, no personnel access -- enforced through both technical and contractual guarantees.
Agent Studio enforces least privilege for LLMs: each agent is configured with access only to the specific Object Types, properties, and actions needed for its task.
AIP supports private model deployments for customers who want retraining, with full governance tools for auditing, interrogating, and monitoring model performance -- separate from third-party models.
Geographic restrictions and regional endpoints help satisfy data residency requirements, with specific regions (US, UK, EU) available depending on model provider capabilities.

title: Workshop - AIP widgets - AIP Agent - Palantir Docs

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's operational application builder that hosts the AIP Agent widget for embedding interactive AI assistants directly into workflow applications.

extractedEntities.name: Agent Studio
extractedEntities.description: Palantir product where AIP Agents are configured with tools, application variables, and prompts before being embedded into Workshop applications.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Agent Widget
extractedEntities.description: A Workshop widget that embeds an interactive AI assistant configured in Agent Studio, capable of reading/writing Workshop variables, querying objects, and applying actions.

extractedEntities.name: Application Variable Mapping
extractedEntities.description: Configuration that maps Agent Studio application variables to Workshop variables, enabling the AI agent to interact with application state according to read/write permissions.

extractedEntities.name: Auto-Send Message
extractedEntities.description: Workshop capability where changes to a textbox variable from outside the AIP Agent widget automatically trigger a new message to the agent, enabling programmatic agent interaction.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entity that the AIP Agent can traverse, query, filter, and aggregate through default Ontology exploration tools.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Object Type attribute that the AIP Agent can read, filter on, and use for aggregations during Ontology exploration.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology write operation that the AIP Agent can execute within Workshop, enabling AI-driven modifications to objects in the operational context.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: AIP Logic or KNN functions that can be added as tools to the AIP Agent widget, extending the agent's capabilities beyond default Ontology exploration.

extractedEntities.name: Embedded Agent in Operational App
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of embedding an AIP Agent into a Workshop application where it can interact with visualization widgets, application variables, and Ontology data within the user's operational context.

keyInsights: The AIP Agent widget bridges AI capabilities and operational workflows by embedding interactive AI assistants directly into Workshop applications where users are already working.
Application variable mapping creates a bidirectional link between Agent Studio and Workshop: agents can read application context and write back results that update visualizations and other widgets.
The auto-send message feature enables programmatic agent interaction from Workshop events, allowing other widgets and actions to trigger AI agent processing without manual user input.
Default Ontology exploration tools give the agent object traversal, property reading, filtering, and aggregation capabilities, making the Ontology queryable through natural language.
Legacy mode (workshop-configured agents) is being sunset in favor of Agent Studio-configured agents, which provide versioning, metrics, session history, and a dedicated configuration UI.

title: The Context Advantage: How Palantir AIP Operates the Modern Enterprise - Towards AI

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that gains its competitive advantage through Ontology-grounded context rather than model size, enabling accurate enterprise reasoning by passing structured Ontology objects as variables to LLMs.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology entity that carries meaning, relationships, and constraints; when passed to AIP, it provides the structured context that grounds LLM reasoning in enterprise reality.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology relationship connecting objects (e.g., asset to events, shipment to orders) that provides the relational context AIP uses for reasoning.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Object attribute carrying data values that form the factual grounding for AIP reasoning, replacing the need for LLMs to infer context from text alone.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology-Grounded AI Reasoning
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where Ontology objects are passed to AIP Logic as variables that define what the model should consider, keeping reasoning focused, reliable, and grounded in structured enterprise data.

extractedEntities.name: Context Advantage
extractedEntities.description: Architectural principle that AI effectiveness comes not from model capability but from the quality and structure of context provided to the model, with the Ontology serving as the context source.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Ontology computation where AIP Logic reasons within boundaries defined by passed Ontology objects, producing recommendations grounded in structured relationships.

extractedEntities.name: Supply Chain Shortage Resolution
extractedEntities.description: Use case where AIP receives shortage part objects, affected production orders, and supplier performance history to reason about and recommend alternative suppliers.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform where the Ontology lives, structuring data into objects with meaning, relationships, and constraints that provide the grounding layer for AIP.

keyInsights: The 'context advantage' thesis: enterprise AI effectiveness depends not on model size or speed but on the quality of structured context provided to the model -- the Ontology is that context layer.
Ontology objects passed to AIP Logic function as variables analogous to ChatGPT conversation context, but grounded in structured, governed enterprise data with relationships and constraints attached.
AIP does not scan the entire Ontology; it reasons within explicit boundaries defined by the objects passed as inputs, keeping reasoning focused and computationally tractable.
The Ontology provides three layers of context: meaning (what the object represents), relationships (how it connects to other objects), and constraints (what limits or dependencies apply).

The supply chain use case demonstrates that AIP's value comes from passing structured objects (shortage part, production orders, supplier performance) rather than requiring the LLM to infer these relationships from text.

title: Palantir AIP: The Next-Generation Enterprise AI System - From Semantic Ontology to Autonomous Operations - AI Monks (Medium)

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform described as a next-generation enterprise AI system that progresses from semantic Ontology through kinetic operations to autonomous enterprise operations.

extractedEntities.name: Semantic Layer
extractedEntities.description: The foundational Ontology layer where data is structured into meaningful objects with relationships, serving as the knowledge representation that AI systems reason over.

extractedEntities.name: Kinetic Layer
extractedEntities.description: The operational layer where AI reasoning translates into real-world actions through Action Types, enabling the Ontology to drive actual enterprise operations beyond passive data modeling.

extractedEntities.name: Dynamic Layer
extractedEntities.description: The autonomous operations layer where AI agents continuously process events, make decisions, and execute actions with minimal human intervention, building on semantic and kinetic layers.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology entity forming the semantic layer's building blocks, carrying meaning and relationships that AI uses for reasoning.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Object Type attributes that encode the factual knowledge in the semantic layer.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology relationships that create the semantic graph connecting Object Types in the semantic layer.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology write mechanism forming the kinetic layer, translating AI decisions into real-world enterprise operations.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Executable logic that bridges the semantic and kinetic layers, enabling AI-driven reasoning to produce operational outputs.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's core platform providing the data foundations and processing pipelines that underpin all three layers of the AIP architecture.

keyInsights: The Ontology serves three distinct architectural layers in AIP: semantic (knowledge representation), kinetic (operational action), and dynamic (autonomous processing) -- forming a progression from understanding to action.

Most enterprise AI deployments fail because LLMs alone lack understanding; the Ontology provides the structured understanding layer that transforms pattern-matching into mission-critical infrastructure.
The semantic layer (Object Types, Properties, Link Types) is necessary but not sufficient; the kinetic layer (Action Types) is what makes AI operational, and the dynamic layer makes it autonomous.
The progression from semantic to kinetic to dynamic mirrors the enterprise AI maturity model: first model the business, then enable AI-driven actions, then automate the decision-action loop.
The analysis positions AIP as fundamentally different from model-centric approaches because it invests in structured context (Ontology) rather than relying solely on model capability.
